Module com.example.ventana
Package com.example.ventana.model

Class GameState

java.lang.Object
com.example.ventana.model.GameState
All Implemented Interfaces:
IGameState
public class GameState extends Object implements IGameState
The GameState class implements the IGameState interface and is responsible for storing the current state of the game.

Main responsibilities include:

  • Tracking the current level.
  • Managing the time allowed per level.
  • Storing the current word.
  • Resetting the game state to its initial values.

This class acts as a simple data container, separating state management from the game logic. It allows the GameLogic class to manipulate the game state without directly handling its internal details.

Author:
Nerie

      Parameters:
      word - the new word as a String.

    • resetGame

      public void resetGame()
      Resets the game state to its initial values.

      The level is set to 1, the time per level is reset to 20 seconds, and the current word is cleared.

      Specified by:
      resetGame in interface IGameState
